title: Commentary on Policy/Limits Compliance: 03-31-2026
content:
The credit union remains facing multiple compliance gaps with board-approved policy limits, supported by mixed capital and asset-quality data. Asset quality metrics show significant pressure in delinquency and charge-off categories.

Asset Quality:
Delinquent loans/total loans of 2% exceed the limit of <1.5%, while net charge-offs/average loans of 1% also exceed their best practice benchmark of <0.75%. However, classified assets/net worth at 1% remains well within its target threshold.

Capital & Solvency:
Net worth ratio and solvency evaluation remain compliant with policy benchmarks, providing a substantial buffer against potential losses. However, net worth growth of 4% falls below the YoY target of >=5%, indicating an area requiring attention to maintain capital adequacy targets.

Profitability & Growth:
Profitability metrics show mixed results across efficiency and return measures. Operating expenses/average assets at 2% remain within limits, while yield on average loans stands strong at 8%. However, net interest margin of 3% falls below the target level of >=3%, suggesting a need to enhance spread management strategies.

Growth metrics show weakness in loan expansion and membership acquisition. Loan growth declined by -0.31% versus expectations of 5%-10%, while membership growth contracted at -3% against targets of 3%-7%. These figures indicate the institution needs strategic adjustments to drive member acquisition and portfolio diversification.

Interest Rate & Funding:
Liquidity metrics remain below preferred ranges, with cash investments/total assets at 4% falling short of the 8%-15% target. Regular shares as a percentage of total funding stands strong at 57%, while loan-to-total-assets ratio remains weak at 32%. Cost of funds sits above acceptable limits at 2%, and net interest margin continues to lag behind targets.

The credit union's genuine strengths lie in its maintained solvency position, controlled operating expense ratios, and healthy yield on loans. However, the institution faces challenges with delinquency management, insufficient capital growth relative to peers, weak liquidity buffers, and suboptimal funding costs that compress profitability margins.
